description: In October 2010, Gap Inc. abruptly replaced its iconic blue-box logo with a minimalist Helvetica design. The move triggered a massive, organized public backlash on social media, forcing the company to revert to the original logo just six days later.

key_events.description: New logo launched on Gap website; immediate negative sentiment on Twitter
Viral mockery spreads; creation of 'Crap Logo' parody site
Gap issues official statement acknowledging the 'love for the original'
Gap officially scraps the redesign and returns to the classic branding

calibration_notes: A classic case study in brand equity loss and the speed of modern digital sentiment backlash.
